title: pisum sativum subsp. arvense
intro: Pisum sativum subsp. arvense, also known as wild garden pea or spring pea, is a subspecies of the garden pea plant and is characterized by its climbing habit and adaptation to growing in a variety of environments. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to support kidney function and act as a natural diuretic, helping to reduce fluid retention and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Pisum sativum subsp. arvense include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve blood sugar regulation, and provide antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
